task.title: Memory Recall relevance and visible-history deduplication
task.source: Direct maintainer report reproduced on the Pi
task.classification: prompt assembly and canonical memory ranking

coreClaim: An ordinary group-chat message recalls only query-relevant canonical memories plus intentional pinned records, and never duplicates transcript chunks whose source messages are already visible to the model.

inScope: Transcript-memory provenance exclusion against selected visible history
Older transcript recall outside the visible-history window

claimBoundary.coreClaim: Selected visible transcript source ids are excluded from transcript recall, and scoped canonical index rows require lexical relevance unless pinned.
claimBoundary.riskType: prompt-context duplication, relevance, and legacy compatibility
entrypoints: Imported and automatically captured chat memories
currentPathsOrFormats: Canonical memory records returned by scoped index queries
Transcript memories with messageIds, firstMessageId, and lastMessageId
legacyPathsOrFormats: Imported chat memories without transcript message ids
Read-behind timestamp fallback for recent legacy memory rows
Pinned canonical memories without query overlap
positiveRowsTested: A behave-related Harlequin memory matches the exact reported user query
A pinned unrelated canonical memory remains eligible
An older transcript chunk outside the selected history window remains recallable
An imported old memory without transcript source ids remains recallable
negativeRowsTested: Same-owner grief and Pokemon-card memories do not match the reported query
A transcript chunk sourced from the visible previous assistant line is not injected
Read-behind still excludes the newest source message
groundTruthFacts: The scoped index API receives only scope and returns candidate rows without query similarity
The reported prompt contained one visible transcript line plus ten canonical rows
Four reported canonical rows had zero lexical overlap but still received a synthetic 0.18 semantic score
claimBoundary.userActionCopy: No user action or data migration is required.

contractLaneGate.brokenContract: Prompt assembly treated selected visible dialogue as recallable history and treated scoped index membership as semantic relevance.
contractLaneGate.consumer: Provider-ready generation prompt and Memory Recall attribution UI
contractLaneGate.impliedContract: Recall adds useful context that is absent from visible history; ownership and storage availability narrow candidates but do not establish query relevance.
contractLaneGate.actualEnforcement: Prompt assembly passes selected source messages into transcript exclusion, while canonical ranking requires lexical overlap or pinned status.
contractLaneGate.wrongLaneFixToAvoid: Do not hide truthful attribution counts in the UI or mutate/delete stored memories.
regressionProof: The new focused tests failed on origin/main with four canonical rows and a duplicated transcript block.
The same tests pass after the owner-level selection repairs.
The generation suite, full Vitest suite, and pnpm check pass.

positiveRows.claim: Relevant same-owner canonical memory remains available.
Pinned canonical memory remains available without lexical overlap.
Older transcript recall continues after visible-history compaction.
positiveRows.evidence: The historyLimit 2 regression injects the older moonlit-mask transcript.
The memory-recall end-to-end suite retains the jasmine-tea imported-memory path.

contradictionRows.claim: Index membership alone must not inject unrelated records.
contradictionRows.evidence: The reported-query regression excludes same-owner Columbina and Pokemon-card records.

contradictionRows.claim: Visible dialogue must not be duplicated as recalled transcript.
contradictionRows.evidence: The visible-history regression excludes the previous assistant source id and produces no transcript memory block.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: The existing one-message read-behind timestamp fallback remains active.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: The original memory recall tests and six end-to-end preflight tests pass.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Status, supersession, scope, token budget, and pinned rules remain active.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: All 578 generation tests and 2,251 repository tests pass.

untestedRows: The exact merged image revision and live Pi prompt behavior are verified after merge and deployment.

ownedFacts.description: The live Harlequin reply jumped from zero to eleven recalled items after a short question.
ownedFacts.evidence: The saved Pi prompt attribution contained one transcript Memory 1 and ten canonical memories.

ownedFacts.description: The literal dialogue memory came from the immediately preceding Harlequin message already present in visible chat history.
ownedFacts.evidence: The chat memory record messageIds matched the previous assistant message id.

notes: The maintainer authorized fixing, publishing, merging, and updating the Pi for this reported bug.
The UI count remains unchanged because it was accurately reporting injected prompt context.
